strategies.name: Strategy 1: VWAP + RSI(3) + EMA(8)
strategies.description: Mean-reversion setup. EMA(8) for trend direction, VWAP for session bias, RSI(3) for entry timing. All three must align before a trade fires.

indicators.ema_8: Trend direction. Price above EMA(8) = bullish momentum. Below = bearish.
indicators.vwap: Session bias. Price above VWAP = buyers in control. Below = sellers. Resets midnight UTC.
indicators.rsi_3: Entry timing. Below 30 in uptrend = snap-back setup. Above 70 in downtrend = reversal likely.

long: Price is above VWAP — buyers in control for the session
Price is above EMA(8) — uptrend confirmed
RSI(3) has dropped below 30 — short-term pullback in an uptrend, snap-back likely
short: Price is below VWAP — sellers in control
Price is below EMA(8) — downtrend confirmed
RSI(3) has spiked above 70 — overbought in a downtrend, reversal likely

exit_rules: Exit when RSI(3) crosses back above 50 (for longs) or below 50 (for shorts)
Hard stop: 0.3% below entry for longs, 0.3% above entry for shorts
Take profit at next VWAP touch or EMA(8) cross
risk_rules: Risk maximum 1% of portfolio per trade
Do not trade if price is more than 1.5% away from VWAP — overextended

strategies.name: Strategy 2: EMA Crossover + MACD + RSI(14)
strategies.description: Trend-following momentum strategy. EMA 21/50 crossover for trend direction, MACD histogram for momentum confirmation, RSI(14) as health filter. ATR-based dynamic stops instead of fixed percentages.

indicators.ema_21: Fast trend line. When above EMA(50) = bullish trend established.
indicators.ema_50: Slow trend line. Acts as dynamic support/resistance.
indicators.rsi_14: Momentum health filter. 40-70 for longs, 30-60 for shorts. Avoids chasing extremes.
indicators.macd_12_26_9: Momentum confirmation. Histogram positive = bullish momentum building. Negative = bearish.
indicators.atr_14: Volatility measure. Used for dynamic stop-loss sizing (1.5x ATR from entry).

long: EMA(21) is above EMA(50) — uptrend established
Price is above EMA(21) — riding the trend, not chasing
RSI(14) is between 40-70 — healthy bullish momentum, not overbought
MACD histogram is positive — momentum confirmed and building
short: EMA(21) is below EMA(50) — downtrend established
Price is below EMA(21) — riding the trend down
RSI(14) is between 30-60 — healthy bearish momentum, not oversold
MACD histogram is negative — bearish momentum confirmed

exit_rules: Exit when price crosses back below EMA(21) for longs, above for shorts
Dynamic stop loss: 1.5x ATR(14) from entry price
Take profit when MACD histogram reverses direction
risk_rules: Risk maximum 1% of portfolio per trade
Do not enter if ATR is unusually low — low volatility means choppy conditions

global_risk_rules: Maximum trade size capped by MAX_TRADE_SIZE_USD in .env
Maximum trades per day capped by MAX_TRADES_PER_DAY in .env
Paper trade first — validate both strategies before going live
notes: Strategy 1 is mean-reversion (catches pullbacks in trends). Strategy 2 is trend-following (rides momentum). They fire in different market conditions — running both gives diversification.
